commit.message:
fix: deep-copy QV event data to prevent access violation after SendMessage returns

The QV DLL sends events via SendMessage, which means the data pointed to by
lparam is only valid during the window proc callback. Previously we stored the
raw pointer and dereferenced it later, causing 0xC0000005 (Access Violation).

Now qv_wnd_proc deep-copies all event data (LoginInfo, OutDataBlock payloads)
into vectors owned by the event queue entry. Consumers read from the copied
buffers instead of stale pointers.

commit.message:
feat: Add raw structured JSON tool_data to DM message pipeline [Midtown !2098] (#1825)

* feat: Add raw structured JSON tool_data to DM message pipeline

Extend the Message struct with `tool_data: Option<Vec<ToolBlock>>` and
`provider: Option<String>` fields so the Svelte client can render
tool-specific UI (syntax-highlighted diffs, collapsible bash output,
todo checklists) instead of receiving pre-formatted markdown.

Key changes:
- Add ToolBlock struct (tool_name, input, output, error) to message.rs
- Add detect_provider() and extract_tool_blocks() to stream.rs
- Modify process_coworker_output() to populate tool_data on DM messages
- Extend PostToChannel effect to carry tool_data and provider
- Pass tool_data/provider through effects.rs executor to Message
- Include tool_data and provider in channel.read RPC responses
- Add tool_data/provider to ChannelMessageData (WebSocket responses)
- Backward compatible: old messages without tool_data still work
- Keep render_tool_call_markdown() as content fallback for non-web clients

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: Remove unnecessary #[allow(dead_code)] from PostToChannel fields

The tool_data and provider fields are actively used in stream.rs
(construction) and effects.rs (destructuring + assignment to Message).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: Fix formatting in rpc_channel.rs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Create Unit 10.6 Capstone Project and Certification Preparation (300+ lines)

- MedTech Solutions ISC Implementation (Capstone Project)
  * Healthcare SaaS company: 1,200 employees, 150 customers, 500,000+ users
  * Business drivers: Patient safety, HIPAA compliance, scale, cost efficiency
  * Challenges addressed:
    - Manual provisioning (3-5 days) → automated (< 2 hours)
    - No deprovisioning automation (security risk) → automated within 4 hours
    - 12-week compliance audits → 2-week audits
    - Growing customer base straining operations

- Solution Design & Implementation Plan
  * Architecture: Hybrid (internal + multi-tenant for customers)
  * 12-week phased implementation (4 phases)
  * Phase 1: Planning & Design (Weeks 1-2)
  * Phase 2: Build & Configure (Weeks 3-6) - 30+ connectors
  * Phase 3: Testing & Validation (Weeks 7-8) - Pilot + comprehensive testing
  * Phase 4: Rollout & Stabilization (Weeks 9-12) - 3-wave production migration

- Success Metrics & Business Impact
  * Operational: 96% faster provisioning, 99% support cost reduction
  * Compliance: 83% faster audits, HIPAA-ready
  * Business: $18.6M annual savings, $70M+ Year 1 ROI

- ISC Certification Exam Preparation
  * Exam overview (120 min, 60-80 questions, 70% pass)
  * Study guide for all 6 domains:
    - Domain 1: ISC Fundamentals (15%)
    - Domain 2: Identity Governance (20%)
    - Domain 3: Access Management (25%)
    - Domain 4: System Connectors (20%)
    - Domain 5: Enterprise Architecture (20%)
  * 10+ practice questions with explanations
  * Pre-exam preparation checklist
  * Career paths and continuing education

commit.message:
fix: security hardening — config permissions, WS payload limit, agentId guard (#64)

Addresses Sprint 1 security issues:

- #58 [C-01+H-03]: Write config files with mode 0o600 (owner-only read/write)
  in saveConfig(), atomicWrite(), and backup. Redact agent_token in
  admin show (first 4 chars + ***).

- #59 [H-01]: Set WebSocket maxPayload to 1 MB to prevent OOM from
  oversized messages. Add 50 KB content length check on DM and
  thread mention handlers.

- #60 [H-04]: Skip org setup (continue) when agent_id is null/missing
  instead of just warning. Without agent_id, isSelf() always returns
  false and self-messages loop back indefinitely.

Closes #58, closes #59, closes #60

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix: Usage API 429 에러 해결 - OAuth 토큰 자동 갱신 구현

만료된 OAuth access token으로 인한 지속적 429 Rate Limit 에러를
해결하기 위해 토큰 자동 갱신 로직을 구현합니다.

- _ensure_valid_token(): expiresAt 확인 후 만료 5분 전 갱신 트리거
- _refresh_token(): platform.claude.com 토큰 엔드포인트로 갱신
- _update_credentials(): 갱신된 토큰을 credentials 파일에 저장
- User-Agent 동적 감지 (하드코딩 2.0.32 → 실제 CLI 버전)
- 에러 가시성 개선 (warmup 에러 로깅, 캐시 히트 디버그 로깅)

Expand Unit 10.5 with comprehensive enterprise case studies (500+ lines)

- Case Study 1: GlobalBank (Fortune 500, 25,000 users, federated architecture)
  * Challenge: 100+ legacy systems, 30-day provisioning, 5% compliance violations
  * Solution: Federated 4-region ISC architecture, 18-month phased migration
  * Results: 93% faster provisioning, 96% compliance violation reduction
  * ROI: 420% over 3 years, $30M+ net benefit

- Case Study 2: TechStartup SaaS (2,000 employees, 500 customers, multi-tenant)
  * Challenge: Manual scaling bottleneck, 2-week customer onboarding
  * Solution: Hybrid multi-tenant ISC, automated onboarding workflow
  * Results: 93% faster onboarding (2 weeks → 2 days), 90% support reduction
  * ROI: $3M annual profit increase, enabled 5x growth without 5x staff

- Common Success Factors Analysis:
  * Executive sponsorship
  * Clear governance model
  * Phased approach
  * Change management
  * Metrics and dashboards
  * Automation
  * Testing and validation

- Implementation best practices extracted from both case studies
- Comparative summary and application guidance
- Metrics and ROI calculation examples
- How to apply lessons to your organization

commit.message:
feat(api): add file-events endpoint and client method

Pipeline tasks need an API surface to report file changes so the
RecordFlow engine can dispatch file flows in the background.

- POST /patients/{id}/file-events accepts a list of changed file names
  and dispatches handle_file_update() via BackgroundTasks
- ClarinetClient.notify_file_changes() wraps the new endpoint

  Redis maxmemory 512mb with LRU eviction

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pipeline): add file change detection to pipeline tasks

Pipeline tasks need to detect when they modify project-level files so
that RecordFlow file flows can react (e.g. invalidating stale records).

The @pipeline_task wrapper now computes SHA256 checksums before and after
task execution for all files accessed via ctx.files (resolve/exists/glob).
Changed files are reported to POST /patients/{id}/file-events.

Key changes:
- FileResolver tracks accessed files in _accessed_files dict
- snapshot_checksums() computes pre-task checksums for all registered files
- _detect_file_changes() compares pre/post checksums for accessed files
- Notification failure is logged but does not fail the task

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Split FOH/BOH into separate department sheets

FOH and BOH buttons now open focused single-department sheets with just
that department's content (context card, prompts, entertainment/culinary).
No step indicator or navigation — just the department form with auto-save
and a Done button. Clicking the inline banner card opens the full stepper
for shared modules (Revenue, Comps, Incidents, Coaching, Guest, Review).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add secure OTP login with enterprise-grade security measures

Implements passwordless SMS OTP login for member portal activation, eliminating the need for admin-sent temporary passwords. Members can now self-activate their accounts using SMS verification codes.

**New Features:**
- OTP login option on member login page (6-digit SMS codes)
- Self-service member portal activation (no admin action needed)
- Automatic password setup prompt for first-time OTP users
- Members can use OTP, password, or biometric login interchangeably

**Security Measures Implemented:**
- Rate limiting: 3 OTP requests per phone per 15 minutes
- Rate limiting: 10 OTP requests per IP per hour
- Account lockout: 5 failed verifications in 15 min = 15-min lockout
- Audit logging: All OTP requests, verifications, and failures logged with IP/user agent
- IP address and user agent tracking for forensic analysis
- Single-use codes with 10-minute expiration

**Technical Changes:**
- Added OTP input view to login page with resend functionality
- Updated MemberAuthContext.verifyOTP() to return needsPasswordSetup flag
- Enhanced send-phone-otp API with phone-based and IP-based rate limiting
- Enhanced verify-phone-otp API with account lockout protection
- Added comprehensive audit logging for security events
- Password hash check in OTP verification for conditional password setup

**User Experience:**
- First-time login: OTP → verify → set password → dashboard
- Returning login: OTP or password (user's choice)
- No waiting for admin to send credentials
- Instant account activation via SMS

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix subscription pricing calculation - remove backwards logic and fallbacks

- Fix backwards calculation in both admin and member portal that was subtracting secondary member fees from base price
- Remove all fallback logic - now returns errors if Stripe API data unavailable
- Member portal API returns 500 if Stripe subscription exists but pricing can't be fetched
- Member portal API returns 404 if account has no stripe_subscription_id
- Admin component shows error state (baseMRR=0) if Stripe API fails instead of using monthly_dues fallback
- Note: monthly_dues in DB is the BASE price from Stripe, not total (set by sync script)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
feat(recordflow): add file-level trigger DSL and engine support

Enable workflows triggered by project-level file changes, so that
downstream records can be automatically invalidated when shared files
(e.g. master models) are updated by pipeline tasks.

Key components:
- FlowFileRecord class with chainable DSL (on_update, invalidate_all_records, call)
- file() factory function accepting .name objects or strings → FILE_REGISTRY
- Engine routing: register_flow() dispatches FlowFileRecord to file_flows dict
- handle_file_update() + _invalidate_by_file() + _call_file_function()
- Loader clears FILE_REGISTRY alongside RECORD/ENTITY registries

Add scheduled priorities with inheritance & override (#31)

* Add scheduled priorities: boost tasks in Today's 5 by day-of-week or date

Tasks can now be scheduled for specific days (weekly recurring or one-off
dates) via the overflow menu or task card long-press. Scheduled tasks get
a 2.5x weight boost in Today's 5 selection. Non-leaf schedules propagate
to all leaf descendants via recursive CTE. Expired one-off schedules are
auto-cleaned. Full Firestore sync support included.

* WIP: Fix pull reconciliation race condition & undo schedule loss

P1: pull() no longer deletes locally-queued entities pending push.
Added getPendingSyncAddKeys() to check sync_queue before reconciliation.

P2: Undo after delete now restores schedules (previously lost to
ON DELETE CASCADE). All 3 delete paths capture schedules, both
restore paths accept and re-insert them.

Also refactored deleteTaskWithRelationships return type from
Map<String, List<int>> to named record for type safety.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add schedule inheritance with override semantics

Child tasks inherit ancestor schedules via recursive CTE, with override
barriers: a task with its own schedule (or is_schedule_override flag)
blocks propagation from above. Dialog shows source attribution
("Inherited from: X") and supports clear override to restore inheritance.

- Override barrier CTE in getScheduleBoostedLeafIds stops at own-schedule nodes
- New getScheduleSources/getEffectiveScheduleDays/getInheritedScheduleDays methods
- is_schedule_override column (DB v16) for empty override (opt-out) edge case
- Schedule dialog: inherited vs override modes, source labels, clear override
- 13 new tests covering override, multi-parent union, deep barriers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: 项目大重构 - 简化为纯粹的爬取和汇总工具

## 核心变更

### 1. 功能简化
- 移除所有 AI 相关功能（提示词生成、Claude Code 集成）
- 项目职责简化为：爬取原始数据 + 周期汇总
- 不再进行 AI 提炼和翻译

### 2. 文件重命名
- main_incremental.py → collect_raw_updates.py（爬取原始数据）
- generate_report_prompt.py → compile_summary.py（周期汇总）
- 内部函数和变量命名统一更新

### 3. 代码优化
- 修复 compile_summary.py 中重复标题的问题
- 优化 README 说明：默认周期为1天，无需参数
- 日期过滤使用 date 对象，避免时分秒干扰

### 4. 删除内容
- generate_report_prompt.py（AI 提示词生成器）
- .claude/skills/tracking-report/SKILL.md（Skill 定义）
- config/prompts.json（AI 提示词配置）

### 5. 更新 .gitignore
- 添加 data/summary/ 到忽略列表（生成的汇总文件）

## 新的项目结构

```
数据流：
爬取：python collect_raw_updates.py → data/raw/*.md
汇总：python compile_summary.py → data/summary/*.md
```

## 使用方式

```bash
# 爬取最新数据
python collect_raw_updates.py

# 生成汇总（默认最近1天）
python compile_summary.py

# 生成其他周期
python compile_summary.py --days 7    # 周报
python compile_summary.py --days 28   # 月报
```

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
